parsed_review.summary: CP00-937 is a clean descriptor-only HRX CandidateFixture-tail + LeaveFixture-foundation bridge pack (RP30.P05.M02.S09-RP30.P05.M03.S09, risk A, 10 units = 1 CandidateFixture S09 tail row + 9 LeaveFixture S01-S09 foundation rows), and its modest claim (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, descriptor_only) is fully supported. HRX_CP937_PACK_BINDING, HRX_CP937_REQUIRED_SECTION_ROWS, and HRX_CP937_REQUIREMENTS (registry.js) match the live closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-937 entry and the manifest included_units exactly, with deviation_from_plan=false and next=CP00-938/RP30.P05.M04.S01. The createHrxCp937CandidateLeaveFixtureBridgeDescriptor factory and createHrxDomainRows hold every runtime/record-write/real-data/permission-bypass/request-runtime/identity-link flag at false, spread HRX_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and HRX_BLOCKED_CLAIMS; validators.js (validateHrxCp937CandidateLeaveFixtureBridgeCoverage/Descriptor and validateHrxPeopleContract) enforces these boundaries and three CP937 tests in model.test.js assert binding, coverage, runtime-closed descriptor, and contract projection. The regenerated contracts/hrx-people-contract.json pins CP00-937 as latest_pack/latest_projection (HrxCp937CandidateLeaveFixtureBridgeDescriptor) with validation.valid=true and a full no_write_attestation. Authority boundary is correct: Claude is read-only and not promoted to final approval (claude_packet.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, c30_read_only=true), human approval is required for any runtime opening (deferred to CP00-938), and the Hermes packet is deterministic review metadata (emits_runtime_receipt=false, deterministic_only=true), not embedded product runtime. Review-evidence hardening passes: readiness auth-preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1228-byte valid JSON ok:true, no auth failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output), prompt is 2397B < 25600B with no full diff/source, review-schema present, only Read/Grep/Glob tools, and no active untracked implementation files omitted. Changed-file scope is respected: unrelated dirty/untracked files (progress-control-room UI, workbook matter docs, ldip review result) are segregated and not attributed to this pack. blocks_goal_closeout=false because the pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust, so the expected human/enterprise-trust approval authority boundary lies outside this receipt. Provenance: this verdict rests on direct read-only source inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) as the packet mandates; the full npm validation ladder is orchestrator-run and recorded as command evidence (HRX syntax checks, node --test, npm run rp30:hrx:validate all exit 0), not re-executed in this review. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
